children.title: Short title, extent and commencement.
children.text:
Short title, extent and commencement.
(1) This Act may be called the Whistle Blowers Protection Act, 2011.
(2) It extends to the whole of India except the State of Jammu and Kashmir.
(3) It shall come into force on such date as the Central Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, appoint; and different dates may be appointed for different provisions of this Act and any reference in any provision to the commencement of this Act shall be construed as a reference to the coming into force of that provision.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Act not to apply to Special Protection Group.
children.text:
Act not to apply to Special Protection Group.
Nothing contained in this Act shall apply to the Special Protection Group constituted under the Special Protection Group Act, 1988 (34 of 1988).
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Definitions.
Public Interest Disclosure to a Competent Authority
Public interest disclosure before Competent Authority.
children.text:
Public interest disclosure before Competent Authority.
(1) Notwithstanding anything contained in the provisions of the Official Secrets Act, 1923 (19 of 1923), any public servant or any other person including any non-governmental organisation may make a public interest disclosure before the Competent Authority.
(2) Every public interest disclosure shall be made in writing or by electronic mail or electronic mail message in such manner as may be prescribed.
(3) Every disclosure shall, on the one hand, contain the identity of the complainant and, on the other hand, the disclosure shall contain full particulars and be accompanied by supporting documents, or other material.
(4) No action shall be taken by the Competent Authority if the disclosure does not indicate the identity of the complainant or the disclosure does not contain full particulars and is not accompanied by supporting documents or other material.
(5) The Competent Authority shall not entertain or inquire into any disclosure—
(a) in respect of which a formal and public inquiry has been ordered under the Public Servants (Inquiries) Act, 1850 (37 of 1850) or under the Commissions of Inquiry Act, 1952 (60 of 1952); or
(b) if the disclosure is, in the opinion of the Competent Authority, frivolous or vexatious or if the identity of the complainant is not revealed or the Competent Authority is of the opinion that the allegations do not constitute any disclosure.
(6) The Competent Authority shall not reveal the identity of the complainant except to the head of the department if the Competent Authority considers it necessary to do so.
(7) It shall not be permissible for the Competent Authority to inquire into any disclosure involving an allegation, if the allegation has been made five years before the date on which the disclosure is received by the Competent Authority.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Powers of Competent Authority.
Discreet inquiry against complainant.
children.text:
Discreet inquiry against complainant.
(1) If the Competent Authority, on receipt of a disclosure or on the basis of information available, is of the opinion that the complainant has filed a false or frivolous disclosure, it shall direct the Head of the department or organisation concerned to conduct a discreet inquiry against the complainant.
(2) The inquiry referred to in sub-section (1) shall be completed within a period of ninety days from the date on which the direction was issued.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Power to call for information.
children.text:
Power to call for information.
The Competent Authority shall have the power to call upon any public servant or person to furnish any information or produce any document relevant to the inquiry being conducted by it under this Act.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Disposal of disclosure.
children.text:
Disposal of disclosure.
The Competent Authority shall complete the inquiry within a period as may be prescribed and shall submit a report of its findings, together with such recommendations as it considers necessary, to the public authority or public servant's appointing authority within a period as may be prescribed.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Action on report of inquiry.
children.text:
Action on report of inquiry.
The public authority or the public servant's appointing authority shall, within a period of three months from the date of receipt of the report, take necessary action on the findings and recommendations of the Competent Authority and intimate the Competent Authority of the action taken.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Competent Authority to protect complainant.
children.text:
Competent Authority to protect complainant.
(1) The Competent Authority may, while inquiring into any disclosure, issue appropriate directions to the concerned public servant or the public authority to protect the complainant.
(2) The Competent Authority may take steps to protect the witnesses and any other person who renders assistance in the inquiry.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Provisions against victimisation.
children.text:
Provisions against victimisation.
(1) No person shall be victimised for having made a disclosure or for having rendered assistance in an inquiry under this Act.
(2) The Competent Authority may give suitable directions to the concerned public servant or the public authority to protect the complainant or the witnesses against any victimisation.
(3) Any person aggrieved by any action taken in violation of sub-section (1) may file a complaint before the Competent Authority and the Competent Authority shall take appropriate action to redress the grievance.
(4) The Competent Authority may, after hearing the complainant and the public authority, pass such orders as it deems fit to protect the complainant.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Power of Competent Authority regarding victimisation.
children.text:
Power of Competent Authority regarding victimisation.
(1) If the Competent Authority is satisfied that any public servant has been victimised, it may recommend to the public authority that the public servant shall be placed in the same position, or as near as possible, to the position in which such public servant would have been placed, had the victimisation not taken place.
(2) The public authority shall take appropriate action on the recommendation of the Competent Authority within a period of three months.
(3) Where the Competent Authority is of the opinion that the public servant has been victimised, it may direct the appropriate authority to take action against the person responsible for the victimisation.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Protection of identity of complainant.
children.text:
Protection of identity of complainant.
(1) The identity of the complainant shall be kept confidential by the Competent Authority and any information which might lead to the identification of the complainant shall not be disclosed except to the head of the department concerned.
(2) No court, tribunal, or authority shall compel the Competent Authority to produce before it any document or reveal the identity of the complainant.
(3) If any person contravenes the provisions of sub-sections (1) and (2), he shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years or with fine which may extend to fifty thousand rupees or with both.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Penalty for false or frivolous disclosure.
children.text:
Penalty for false or frivolous disclosure.
Any person who makes a disclosure malafide or knowingly makes a disclosure which is false or frivolous shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to two years and also with fine which may extend to thirty thousand rupees.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Penalty for failure to comply with directions.
children.text:
Penalty for failure to comply with directions.
Any person who wilfully fails to comply with any direction issued by the Competent Authority under this Act shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year or with fine which may extend to twenty-five thousand rupees or with both.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Penalty for revealing identity of complainant.
children.text:
Penalty for revealing identity of complainant.
Any person who reveals the identity of the complainant in contravention of the provisions of this Act shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years and also with fine which may extend to fifty thousand rupees.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Offences by companies.
children.text:
Offences by companies.
(1) Where an offence under this Act has been committed by a company, every person who, at the time the offence was committed, was in charge of, and was responsible to, the company for the conduct of the business of the company, as well as the company, shall be deemed to be guilty of the offence and shall be liable to be proceeded against and punished accordingly:
Provided that nothing contained in this sub-section shall render any such person liable to any punishment provided in this Act, if he proves that the offence was committed without his knowledge or that he had exercised all due diligence to prevent the commission of such offence.
(2) Notwithstanding anything contained in sub-section (1), where an offence under this Act has been committed by a company and it is proved that the offence has been committed with the consent or connivance of, or is attributable to any neglect on the part of, any director, manager, secretary or other officer of the company, such director, manager, secretary or other officer shall also be deemed to be guilty of that offence and shall be liable to be proceeded against and punished accordingly.
Explanation.— For the purposes of this section,—
(a) "company" means any body corporate and includes a firm or other association of individuals; and
(b) "director", in relation to a firm, means a partner in the firm.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Complaints against Prime Minister.
children.text:
Complaints against Prime Minister.
(1) No action shall be taken by the Competent Authority on a public interest disclosure against the Prime Minister unless the full bench of the Competent Authority considers the disclosure, and a finding is recorded that a prima facie case exists warranting further inquiry.
(2) In case the Competent Authority decides to proceed to inquire into the disclosure, it shall constitute a committee of three or more members, including a Chairperson who is or has been a Judge of the Supreme Court, to further inquire into the matter.
(3) Record of any such inquiry shall not be published or made available to anyone, except as required by a court of law.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Hearing before taking action.
children.text:
Hearing before taking action.
No action shall be taken by the Competent Authority against any public servant on the basis of a public interest disclosure, unless a reasonable opportunity of being heard has been given to the concerned public servant.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Protection of action taken in good faith.
children.text:
Protection of action taken in good faith.
No suit, prosecution or other legal proceeding shall lie against the Central Government or the State Government or the Competent Authority or any officer of the Government or the Competent Authority in respect of anything which is done or intended to be done in good faith under this Act or the rules made thereunder.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Staff of Competent Authority.
children.text:
Staff of Competent Authority.
The Competent Authority may, for the purposes of performing its functions under this Act, require such number of officers and employees as may be prescribed.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Annual report.
children.text:
Annual report.
The Competent Authority shall, at the end of each year, prepare in such form and at such time as may be prescribed, an annual report giving a summary of the disclosures received and the action taken thereon during the year and forward a copy thereof to the Central Government or the State Government, as the case may be.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Power to make rules.
children.text:
Power to make rules.
(1) The Central Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, make rules for carrying out the provisions of this Act.
(2) In particular, and without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing power, such rules may provide for—
(a) the manner of making a public interest disclosure under sub-section (2) of section 4;
(b) the manner of conducting inquiry under sub-section (1) of section 5;
(c) any other matter which is to be, or may be, prescribed.
(3) Every rule made under this section shall be laid, as soon as may be after it is made, before each House of Parliament.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Act to have overriding effect.
children.text:
Act to have overriding effect.
The provisions of this Act shall have effect notwithstanding anything inconsistent therewith contained in any other law for the time being in force or in any instrument having effect by virtue of any law other than this Act.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

children.title: Power to remove difficulties.
children.text:
Power to remove difficulties.
(1) If any difficulty arises in giving effect to the provisions of this Act, the Central Government may, by order, published in the Official Gazette, make such provisions not inconsistent with the provisions of this Act as may appear to be necessary for removing the difficulty:
Provided that no order shall be made under this section after the expiry of two years from the commencement of this Act.
(2) Every order made under this section shall be laid, as soon as may be after it is made, before each House of Parliament.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
